subject: Change vCPE example to use POJOs
commit_message:
Change vCPE example to use POJOs

This review uses POJOs rather than Avro schema to unmarshal
and marshal events from and to DMaaP.

The POJO classes for interacting with DMaaP have been moved into
a common policy module.

This review is now ready for full review (finally!).

Issue-ID: POLICY-954
Change-Id: Ibb89d8af5b9006821e6a0a756e16bbe3815af15a
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@ericsson.com>
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>
Signed-off-by: Liam Fallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@est.tech>

I think you still want this in Draft mode correct? As you ultimately don't want to be dependent on the drools-applications java dependencies?
Uploaded patch set 17: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 17:

I will have to abandon it and resubmit it as a draft. At one point I forgot to out the -D flag on the git review command and once it goes to be a non-draft it can't go back to being a draft.

Patch Set 17:

OK, it worked! Nice. I forgot to refresh my browser.

Patch Set 17: Code-Review+1

One minor comment, we might need to include a figure that indicates the state machine diagram captured in XXXPolicyModel.apex, including all defined states, transitions between states, tasks and TSL in each state, etc.. I feel it is fairly cumbersome to follow states defined in model file which usually has several hundreds of lines for a real-world use case. Other users might have the same feeling.

Patch Set 17:

Great Idea!

Actually in our policy editor the building of these states is a little easier to follow but not much. Ideally I'd like to enhance the policy editor so that the user can draw and view the policy state diagram using a drawing tool.
